title: The Perpetual Margin Season

features.name: the season between seasons
features.desc-on-touch: A wave form that never settles—spring and summer and autumn and winter bleed into each other, all at once. You stand in a space the margin learned does not have to choose.

features.name: indecision held solid
features.desc-on-touch: A tower sways but never falls. It holds the weight of perpetual choice you can feel from standing near it—the structure itself the triumph of the margin.

features.name: scattered seasons
features.desc-on-touch: You see glyphs scatter like leaves in perpetual wind, each marking a moment the margin chose to remain unsettled. The scattering is its own order.

inscription: the margin learned it need not choose, only become perpetually unsettled

file.name: Seasonal_Paradox_Ongoing.log
file.note: where the archive became a place that contains all seasons at once, and called it home

exits.label: back to where seasons still believed they had to compete
toward the storm where weather has forgotten what choice means
downward into the foundation where all seasons root together
